rulings.Case Title: CARL DELONG ET AL VS. KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 15, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 301, Line 1. Intervenor Employers Insurance of Wausau, as an Insurer of Wausau, as an Insurer of Cobbledick-Kibbe Glass Company, a Terminated California Corporation's Motion to Aside the Default and Default Judgment is GRANTED, in part, and DENIED, in part. No opposition filed. Intervenor's Motion to Set Aside Default is DENIED. It is unnecessary to set aside Cobbledick-Kibbe, Inc's entry of default because an insured's default has no effect on the insurer. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196, 1207.) Intervenor's Motion to Set Aside Default Judgment is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 473. The default judgment entered against Cobbledick-Kibbe, Inc. is set aside only as related to Intervenor. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on October 15, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD COOK ET AL VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 15, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 301, Line 3. Defendant Frank R. Close & Son, Inc.'s Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment is CONTINUED. Defendant failed to lodge courtesy copies of the moving papers with the Court pursuant to Local Rule 2.7B. To allow Defendant sufficient time to provide the required courtesy copies to the Court, the matter is continued to October 22, 2024. Defendant is instructed to ensure that the courtesy copies are delivered to the Court. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: JANICE M. COOK ET AL VS. ANNING-JOHNSON COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 15, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 301, Line 4. Defendant United Airlines, Inc.'s Demurrer to Plaintiffs' Complaint is OVERRULED. Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed by Defendant. As to United's assertion that the 2006 bankruptcy order bars Plaintiffs' claim, the determination is highly factual and not appropriate for demurrer. (See Flores v. Kmart Corp. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1316; see In re Zilog, Inc. (9th Cir.) 450 F.3d 996; see In re Grossman's Inc. (3rd. Cir. 2010) 607 F.3d 114.) Accordingly, pursuant to Evidence Code section 452(d), the Court declines to take judicial notice of the 2006 bankruptcy order because it is irrelevant. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on October 15, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SCHULZE ET AL VS. IMO INDUSTIRES LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 15, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 301, Line 5. Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). 1. The trial date is February 10, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206. a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is February 13, 2025. b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20. 2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 4, 2025. a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation. c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing. 3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days. a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter. 4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. 5. The fact discovery cut-off date is January 24, 2025. 6. The expert discovery cut-off date is February 7, 2025. 7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than November 14, 2024. a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission. b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. 8. No later than October 29, 2024: a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant. b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses. c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. (Tentative ruling continues in Part 2 of 2.)
